1.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
1.text: Ha; Meem. (Alphabets of the Arabic language – Allah, and to whomever He reveals, know their precise meanings.)

1.author: Hilali & Khan
1.text: Ha-Mim. [These letters are one of the miracles of the Quran and none but Allah (Alone) knows their meanings].

2.author: Ahmed Ali
2.text: The revelation of this Book is from God, the mighty and all-wise.

2.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
2.text: The sending down of the Book is from Allah, the Most Honourable, the Wise.

2.author: Arberry
2.text: The sending down of the Book is from God, the All-mighty, the All-wise.

2.author: Daryabadi
2.text: The revelation of the Book is from Allah, the Mighty, the Wise.

2.author: Hilali & Khan
2.text: The revelation of the Book (this Quran) is from Allah, the All-Mighty, the All-Wise.

2.author: Itani
2.text: The revelation of the Book is from God, the Exalted in Might, the Wise.

2.author: Maududi
2.text: This Book is a revelation from the Most Mighty, the Most Wise.

2.author: Mubarakpuri
2.text: The revelation of the Book is from Allah, the Almighty, the All-Wise.

2.author: Pickthall
2.text: The revelation of the Scripture is from Allah, the Mighty, the Wise.

2.author: Qarai
2.text: The [gradual] sending down of the Book is from Allah, the All-mighty, All-wise.

2.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
2.text: The sending down of the Book is from Allah, the Almighty, the Wise.

2.author: Saheeh International
2.text: The revelation of the Book is from Allah, the Exalted in Might, the Wise.

2.author: Sarwar
2.text: This Book is revealed from God, the Majestic and All-wise.

2.author: Shakir
2.text: The revelation of the Book is from Allah, the Mighty, the Wise.

2.author: Wahiduddin Khan
2.text: This Scripture is sent down from God, the Mighty and Wise One.

2.author: Yusuf Ali
2.text: The revelation of the Book is from Allah the Exalted in Power, Full of Wisdom.

2.author: Transliteration
2.text: Tanzeelu alkitabi mina Allahi alAAazeezi alhakeemi

3.author: Ahmed Ali
3.text: Indeed there are signs for believers in the heavens and the earth.

3.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
3.text: Indeed in the heavens and the earth are signs for believers.

3.author: Arberry
3.text: Surely in the heavens and earth there are signs for the believers;

3.author: Daryabadi
3.text: Verily in the heavens and the earth are signs Unto the believers.

3.author: Hilali & Khan
3.text: Verily, in the heavens and the earth are signs for the believers.

3.author: Itani
3.text: In the heavens and the earth are proofs for the believers.

3.author: Maududi
3.text: Behold, for those who believe there are (myriad) Signs in the heavens and the earth

3.author: Mubarakpuri
3.text: Verily, in the heavens and the earth are signs for the believers.

3.author: Pickthall
3.text: Lo! in the heavens and the earth are portents for believers.

3.author: Qarai
3.text: In the heavens and the earth there are indeed signs for the faithful,

3.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
3.text: Indeed, there are signs in the heavens and earth for believers,

3.author: Saheeh International
3.text: Indeed, within the heavens and earth are signs for the believers.

3.author: Sarwar
3.text: In the heavens and the earth there is evidence (of the Truth) for the believers.

3.author: Shakir
3.text: Most surely in the heavens and the earth there are signs for the believers.

3.author: Wahiduddin Khan
3.text: There are signs in the heavens and the earth for those who believe:

3.author: Yusuf Ali
3.text: Verily in the heavens and the earth, are Signs for those who believe.

3.author: Transliteration
3.text: Inna fee alssamawati waalardi laayatin lilmumineena

4.author: Ahmed Ali
4.text: In creating you and spreading all the moving things (on earth) are signs for people firm of faith,

4.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
4.text: And in your creation, and all the creatures He scatters in the earth – in them are signs for the people who are certain.

4.author: Arberry
4.text: and in your creation, and the crawling things He scatters abroad, there are signs for a people having sure faith,

4.author: Daryabadi
4.text: And in the creation of yourselves and of the beasts that He hath scattered over the earth, are signs Unto a people who are convinced.

4.author: Hilali & Khan
4.text: And in your creation, and what He scattered (through the earth) of moving (living) creatures are signs for people who have Faith with certainty.

4.author: Itani
4.text: And in your own creation, and in the creatures He scattered, are signs for people of firm faith.

4.author: Maududi
4.text: and in your own creation; and in the animals which He spreads out over the earth too there are Signs for those endowed with sure faith;

4.author: Mubarakpuri
4.text: And in your creation, and what He spread (through the earth) of moving creatures are signs for people who have faith with certainty.

4.author: Pickthall
4.text: And in your creation, and all the beasts that He scattereth in the earth, are portents for a folk whose faith is sure.

4.author: Qarai
4.text: There are signs for people who have certainty in your own creation and in animals, He scatters abroad.

4.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
4.text: and in your creation, and in the crawling things that He has scattered far and near, there are signs for a nation whose belief is certain,

4.author: Saheeh International
4.text: And in the creation of yourselves and what He disperses of moving creatures are signs for people who are certain [in faith].

4.author: Sarwar
4.text: In your creation and in that of the beasts living on earth there is evidence of the Truth for the people who have strong faith.

4.author: Shakir
4.text: And in your (own) creation and in what He spreads abroad of animals there are signs for a people that are sure;

4.author: Wahiduddin Khan
4.text: in your own creation and all the creatures He has spread about, there are signs for people of sure faith;

4.author: Yusuf Ali
4.text: And in the creation of yourselves and the fact that animals are scattered (through the earth), are Signs for those of assured Faith.

4.author: Transliteration
4.text: Wafee khalqikum wama yabuththu min dabbatin ayatun liqawmin yooqinoona

5.author: Ahmed Ali
5.text: As there are signs in the alternation of night and day, and in rain that God sends with which He revives the earth once dead, as there are in the changing of the winds, for people who can understand.

5.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
5.text: And in the alternation of night and day, and in the means of sustenance – water – which Allah sends down from the sky whereby He gave life to the earth after its death, and in the movement of the winds – (in all these) are signs for people of intellect.

5.author: Arberry
5.text: and in the alternation of night and day, and the provision God sends down from heaven, and therewith revives the earth after it is dead, and the turning about of the winds, there are signs for a people who understand.

5.author: Daryabadi
5.text: And in the alternation of night and day and that which Allah sendeth down of provision from the heaven and thereby quickeneth the earth after the death thereof and the turning about of the winds, are signs Unto a people who reflect.

5.author: Hilali & Khan
5.text: And in the alternation of night and day, and the provision (rain) that Allah sends down from the sky, and revives therewith the earth after its death, and in the turning about of the winds (i.e. sometimes towards the east or north, and sometimes towards the south or west etc., sometimes bringing glad tidings of rain etc., and sometimes bringing the torment), are signs for a people who understand.

5.author: Itani
5.text: And in the alternation of night and day, and in the sustenance God sends down from the sky, with which He revives the earth after its death, and in the circulation of the winds, are marvels for people who reason.

5.author: Maududi
5.text: and in the succession of night and day, and in the provision that Allah sends down from the sky wherewith He gives life to the earth after it had been lifeless, and in the change of the winds: (in all these) there are Signs for people who use reason.

5.author: Mubarakpuri
5.text: And in the alternation of night and day, and the provision that Allah sends down from the sky, and revives therewith the earth after its death, and in the turning about of the winds, are signs for a people who understand.

5.author: Pickthall
5.text: And the difference of night and day and the provision that Allah sendeth down from the sky and thereby quickeneth the earth after her death, and the ordering of the winds, are portents for a people who have sense.

5.author: Qarai
5.text: There are signs for people who exercise their reason in the alternation of night and day, in the provision that Allah sends down from the sky, with which He revives the earth after its death, and in the changing of the winds.

5.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
5.text: and in the alternation of the night and day, in the provision Allah sends down from heaven with which the earth is revived after its death, and in the changing about of the winds, there are signs for people who understand.

5.author: Saheeh International
5.text: And [in] the alternation of night and day and [in] what Allah sends down from the sky of provision and gives life thereby to the earth after its lifelessness and [in His] directing of the winds are signs for a people who reason.

5.author: Sarwar
5.text: In the alternation of the night and the day, the sustenance which God has sent down from the sky to revive the barren earth, and in the changing of the direction of the winds there is evidence of the truth for the people of understanding.

5.author: Shakir
5.text: And (in) the variation of the night and the day, and (in) what Allah sends down of sustenance from the cloud, then gives life thereby to the earth after its death, and (in) the changing of the winds, there are signs for a people who understand.

5.author: Wahiduddin Khan
5.text: and in the succession of night and day, and in the means of subsistence which God sends down from the skies, giving life thereby to the earth after it had been lifeless, and in the circulation of the winds: [in all this] there are signs for people who use their reason.

5.author: Yusuf Ali
5.text: And in the alternation of Night and Day, and the fact that Allah sends down Sustenance from the sky, and revives therewith the earth after its death, and in the change of the winds,- are Signs for those that are wise.

5.author: Transliteration
5.text: Waikhtilafi allayli waalnnahari wama anzala Allahu mina alssamai min rizqin faahya bihi alarda baAAda mawtiha watasreefi alrriyahi ayatun liqawmin yaAAqiloona

6.author: Ahmed Ali
6.text: These are revelations of God which we recite to you correctly: In what other lore but God and His manifestations would they then believe?

6.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
6.text: These are the verses of Allah which We recite to you with the truth; so forsaking Allah and His signs, what will they believe in?

6.author: Arberry
6.text: Those are the signs of God that We recite to thee in truth; in what manner of discourse then, after God and His signs, will they believe?

6.author: Daryabadi
6.text: These are the revelations of Allah which We rehearse Unto thee with truth: in what discourse then, after Allah and His revelations, will they believe?

6.author: Hilali & Khan
6.text: These are the Ayat (proofs, evidences, verses, lessons, revelations, etc.) of Allah, which We recite to you (O Muhammad SAW) with truth. Then in which speech after Allah and His Ayat will they believe?

6.author: Itani
6.text: These are God’s Verses which We recite to you in truth. In which message, after God and His revelations, will they believe?

6.author: Maududi
6.text: These are Allah's Signs that We rehearse to you in Truth. In what kind of discourse after Allah and His Signs will they, then, believe?

6.author: Mubarakpuri
6.text: These are the Ayat of Allah, which We recite to you with truth. Then in which speech after Allah and His Ayat will they believe

6.author: Pickthall
6.text: These are the portents of Allah which We recite unto thee (Muhammad) with truth. Then in what fact, after Allah and His portents, will they believe?

6.author: Qarai
6.text: These are the signs of Allah that We recite for you in truth. So what discourse will they believe after Allah and His signs?

6.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
6.text: Such are the verses of Allah. We recite them to you in truth. So in what speech, after Allah and His signs, will they believe?

6.author: Saheeh International
6.text: These are the verses of Allah which We recite to you in truth. Then in what statement after Allah and His verses will they believe?

6.author: Sarwar
6.text: These are the revelations of God which We recite to you for a genuine purpose. In what statements other than God's and His revelations will they then believe?

6.author: Shakir
6.text: These are the communications of Allah which We recite to you with truth; then in what announcement would they believe after Allah and His communications?

6.author: Wahiduddin Khan
6.text: These are God's revelations, which We recite to you in all truth. But if they deny God and His revelations, in what message will they believe?

6.author: Yusuf Ali
6.text: Such are the Signs of Allah, which We rehearse to thee in Truth; then in what exposition will they believe after (rejecting) Allah and His Signs?

6.author: Transliteration
6.text: Tilka ayatu Allahi natlooha AAalayka bialhaqqi fabiayyi hadeethin baAAda Allahi waayatihi yuminoona

7.author: Ahmed Ali
7.text: Alas the woe for every dissembling sinner

7.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
7.text: Ruin is for every great slanderer, excessive sinner.

7.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
7.text: Woe to all the guilty impostors!

7.author: Yusuf Ali
7.text: Woe to each sinful dealer in Falsehoods:

8.author: Ahmed Ali
8.text: Who hears the revelations of God being recited to him, yet persists in denying with arrogance as though he had never heard them! So warn him of a painful punishment.

8.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
8.text: Who hears the verses of Allah which are recited to him, then remains stubborn, proud, as if he did not hear them; therefore give him the glad tidings of a painful punishment.

8.author: Arberry
8.text: who hears the signs of God being recited to him, then perseveres in waxing proud, as if he has not heard them; so give him the good tidings of a painful chastisement.

8.author: Daryabadi
8.text: Who heareth the revelations of Allah rehearsed Unto him, and yet persisteth with stiff-neckedness as though he heard them not. Announce thou Unto him, then, a torment afflictive.

8.author: Hilali & Khan
8.text: Who hears the Verses of Allah (being) recited to him, yet persists with pride as if he heard them not. So announce to him a painful torment!

8.author: Itani
8.text: Who hears God's revelations being recited to him, yet he persists arrogantly, as though he did not hear them. Announce to him a painful punishment.

8.author: Maududi
8.text: who hears Allah's Signs being rehearsed to him, and yet persists in his pride, as though he had not heard it. Announce to him, then, the tidings of a grievous chastisement.

8.author: Mubarakpuri
8.text: Who hears the Ayat of Allah Tutla (recited) to him, yet persists with pride as if he heard them not. So announce to him a painful torment!

8.author: Pickthall
8.text: Who heareth the revelations of Allah recited unto him, and then continueth in pride as though he heard them not. Give him tidings of a painful doom.

8.author: Qarai
8.text: who hears the signs of Allah being recited to him, yet persists arrogantly as if he had not heard them. So inform him of a painful punishment.

8.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
8.text: He hears the verses of Allah recited to him and then, as though he never heard them, persists in insolence. Give to him the glad tidings of a painful punishment.

8.author: Saheeh International
8.text: Who hears the verses of Allah recited to him, then persists arrogantly as if he had not heard them. So give him tidings of a painful punishment.

8.author: Sarwar
8.text: He hears the revelations of God which are recited to him, then persists in his arrogance as if he had not even heard them. Tell him that he will suffer a painful torment.

8.author: Shakir
8.text: Who hears the communications of Allah recited to him, then persists proudly as though he had not heard them; so announce to him a painful punishment.

8.author: Wahiduddin Khan
8.text: He hears God's revelations being recited to him, yet persists in his arrogance as if he had never heard them. Forewarn him of a painful punishment.

8.author: Yusuf Ali
8.text: He hears the Signs of Allah rehearsed to him, yet is obstinate and lofty, as if he had not heard them: then announce to him a Penalty Grievous!

8.author: Transliteration
8.text: YasmaAAu ayati Allahi tutla AAalayhi thumma yusirru mustakbiran kaan lam yasmaAAha fabashshirhu biAAathabin aleemin

9.author: Ahmed Ali
9.text: When he comes to know something of Our messages he makes fun of them: For them is shameful punishment.

9.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
9.text: And when he comes to know about any of the verses, he mocks at it; for them is a disgraceful punishment.

9.author: Arberry
9.text: And when he knows anything of Our signs, he takes them in mockery; those for them awaits a humbling chastisement.

9.author: Daryabadi
9.text: And when he cometh to know aught of Our revelations, he taketh it scoffingly. These! theirs shall be a torment ignominous;

9.author: Hilali & Khan
9.text: And when he learns something of Our Verses (this Quran), he makes them a jest. For such there will be a humiliating torment.

9.author: Itani
9.text: And when he learns something of Our revelations, he takes them in mockery. For such there is a shameful punishment.

9.author: Maududi
9.text: Whenever he comes to know anything of Our Signs, he makes them an object of jest. For such there awaits a humiliating chastisement.

9.author: Mubarakpuri
9.text: And when he learns something of Our Ayat, he makes them a jest. For such there will be a humiliating torment.

9.author: Pickthall
9.text: And when he knoweth aught of Our revelations he maketh it a jest. For such there is a shameful doom.

9.author: Qarai
9.text: Should he learn anything about Our signs, he takes them in derision. For such there is a humiliating punishment.

9.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
9.text: As for he who knows something of Our verses and then mocks them for those there is a humiliating punishment.

9.author: Saheeh International
9.text: And when he knows anything of Our verses, he takes them in ridicule. Those will have a humiliating punishment.

9.author: Sarwar
9.text: When he learns about some of Our revelations, he mocks them. Such people will suffer a humiliating torment.

9.author: Shakir
9.text: And when he comes to know of any of Our communications, he takes it for a jest; these it is that shall have abasing chastisement.

9.author: Wahiduddin Khan
9.text: When he learns something of Our revelations, he derides them: for such there will be humiliating torment.

9.author: Yusuf Ali
9.text: And when he learns something of Our Signs, he takes them in jest: for such there will be a humiliating Penalty.

9.author: Transliteration
9.text: Waitha AAalima min ayatina shayan ittakhathaha huzuwan olaika lahum AAathabun muheenun

10.author: Ahmed Ali
10.text: There is Hell before them; and whatever they have earned will not avail them in the least, nor those whom they take as friends apart from God. There is great chastisement for them.

10.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
10.text: Hell is after them; and what they have earned will not benefit them at all, nor those whom they have chosen as supporters besides Allah; and for them is a terrible punishment.

10.author: Arberry
10.text: Behind them Gehenna; and that they have earned shall not avail them aught, nor those they took as protectors, apart from God; for them awaits a mighty chastisement.

10.author: Daryabadi
10.text: Before them is Hell; naught will avail them of that which they earned, nor those whom they took for patrons beside Allah. Theirs shall be a torment mighty.

10.author: Hilali & Khan
10.text: In front of them there is Hell, and that which they have earned will be of no profit to them, nor (will be of any profit to them) those whom they have taken as Auliya' (protectors, helpers, etc.) besides Allah. And theirs will be a great torment.

10.author: Itani
10.text: Beyond them lies Hell. What they have earned will not benefit them at all, nor will those they adopted as lords instead of God. They will have a terrible punishment.

10.author: Maududi
10.text: Hell is behind them. Their worldly earnings shall not avail them, nor those whom they took as protectors instead of Allah. An awesome chastisement lies in store for them.

10.author: Mubarakpuri
10.text: In front of them there is Hell. And that which they have earned will be of no profit to them, nor those whom they have taken as protective friends besides Allah. And theirs will be a great torment.

10.author: Pickthall
10.text: Beyond them there is hell, and that which they have earned will naught avail them, nor those whom they have chosen for protecting friends beside Allah. Theirs will be an awful doom.

10.author: Qarai
10.text: Ahead of them is hell, and neither what they have earned, nor what they had taken as guardians besides Allah will avail them in any way, and there is a great punishment for them.

10.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
10.text: Gehenna (Hell) is behind them, all that they have earned shall not help them at all, nor shall those they took as guardians, other than Allah. For them there is a mighty punishment.

10.author: Saheeh International
10.text: Before them is Hell, and what they had earned will not avail them at all nor what they had taken besides Allah as allies. And they will have a great punishment.

10.author: Sarwar
10.text: Hell is awaiting them and none of their deeds will be of any benefit to them, nor will any guardian whom they have chosen besides God. They will suffer a great torment.

10.author: Shakir
10.text: Before them is hell, and there shall not avail them aught of what they earned, nor those whom they took for guardians besides Allah, and they shall have a grievous punishment.

10.author: Wahiduddin Khan
10.text: In front of them is Hell; and of no profit to them is anything they may have earned, nor any protectors they may have taken to themselves besides God: for them there shall be a terrible punishment.

10.author: Yusuf Ali
10.text: In front of them is Hell: and of no profit to them is anything they may have earned, nor any protectors they may have taken to themselves besides Allah: for them is a tremendous Penalty.

10.author: Transliteration
10.text: Min waraihim jahannamu wala yughnee AAanhum ma kasaboo shayan wala ma ittakhathoo min dooni Allahi awliyaa walahum AAathabun AAatheemun

11.author: Ahmed Ali
11.text: This is guidance. Those who deny the revelations of their Lord will suffer a dreadful doom.

11.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
11.text: This is the (true) guidance; and for those who disbelieve in the verses of their Lord is the severest of painful punishments.

11.author: Arberry
11.text: This is guidance; and those who disbelieve in the signs of their Lord, there awaits them a painful chastisement of wrath.

11.author: Daryabadi
11.text: This Book is guidance, and those who disbelieve in the revelations of their Lord, theirs shall be torment of calamity, afflictive.

11.author: Hilali & Khan
11.text: This (Quran) is a guidance. And those who disbelieve in the Ayat (proofs, evidences, verses, lessons, signs, revelations, etc.) of their Lord, for them there is a painful torment of Rijz (a severe kind of punishment).

11.author: Itani
11.text: This is guidance. Those who blaspheme their Lord’s revelations will have a punishment of agonizing pain.

11.author: Maududi
11.text: This (Qur'an) is the true guidance. Those who deny the Signs of their Lord shall suffer the torment of a woeful scourge.

11.author: Mubarakpuri
11.text: This is guidance. And those who disbelieve in the Ayat of their Lord, for them there is a painful torment of Rijz.

11.author: Pickthall
11.text: This is guidance. And those who disbelieve the revelations of their Lord, for them there is a painful doom of wrath.

11.author: Qarai
11.text: This is [true] guidance, and there is a painful punishment due to defilement for those who defy the signs of their Lord.

11.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
11.text: This is guidance; for those who disbelieve in the verses of their Lord the anger of the painful punishment awaits them.

11.author: Saheeh International
11.text: This [Qur'an] is guidance. And those who have disbelieved in the verses of their Lord will have a painful punishment of foul nature.

11.author: Sarwar
11.text: This (Quran) is a guidance. Those who reject the revelations of their Lord will suffer the most painful punishment.

11.author: Shakir
11.text: This is guidance; and (as for) those who disbelieve in the communications of their Lord, they shall have a painful punishment on account of uncleanness.

11.author: Wahiduddin Khan
11.text: Such is Our guidance; those who reject their Lord's revelations shall suffer a woeful punishment.

11.author: Yusuf Ali
11.text: This is (true) Guidance and for those who reject the Signs of their Lord, is a grievous Penalty of abomination.

11.author: Transliteration
11.text: Hatha hudan waallatheena kafaroo biayati rabbihim lahum AAathabun min rijzin aleemin

12.author: Ahmed Ali
12.text: It is God who subjugated the ocean for you so that ships may ply through it by His command, and you may seek His bounty, and may haply be grateful.

12.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
12.text: It is Allah Who has subjected the sea for you so that ships may sail upon it by His command, and for you to seek His munificence, and so that you may give thanks.

12.author: Arberry
12.text: God is He who has subjected to you the sea, that the ships may run on it at His commandment, and that you may seek His bounty; haply so you will be thankful.

12.author: Daryabadi
12.text: Allah it is Who hath subjected the sea for your sake, that the ships may run thereon by His command and that ye may seek of His grace, and that haply ye may return thanks.

12.author: Hilali & Khan
12.text: Allah it is He Who has subjected to you the sea, that ships may sail through it by His Command, and that you may seek of His Bounty, and that you may be thankful,

12.author: Itani
12.text: It is God who placed the sea at your service, so that ships may run through it by His command, and that you may seek of His bounty, and that you may give thanks.

12.author: Maududi
12.text: Allah it is Who has subjected the sea to you so that ships may sail upon it at His bidding and you may seek of His Bounty and give thanks to Him.

12.author: Mubarakpuri
12.text: Allah, it is He Who has subjected to you the sea, that ships may sail through it by His command, and that you may seek of His bounty, and that you may be thankful.

12.author: Pickthall
12.text: Allah it is Who hath made the sea of service unto you that the ships may run thereon by His command, and that ye may seek of His bounty, and that haply ye may be thankful;

12.author: Qarai
12.text: It is Allah who disposed the sea for you[r benefit] so that the ships may sail in it by His command, that you may seek of His bounty and that you may give thanks.

12.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
12.text: It is Allah who has subjected to you the sea so that ships run upon it at His command, and so that you may seek His bounty and be thankful.

12.author: Saheeh International
12.text: It is Allah who subjected to you the sea so that ships may sail upon it by His command and that you may seek of His bounty; and perhaps you will be grateful.

12.author: Sarwar
12.text: God has made the sea subservient to you so that ships sail on by His command and you seek His favors. Perhaps you will be grateful.

12.author: Shakir
12.text: Allah is He Who made subservient to you the sea that the ships may run therein by His command, and that you may seek of His grace, and that you may give thanks.

12.author: Wahiduddin Khan
12.text: It is God who has subjected the sea to you so that you may sail thereon by His command, and so that you may seek His bounty, and so that you may be grateful.

12.author: Yusuf Ali
12.text: It is Allah Who has subjected the sea to you, that ships may sail through it by His command, that ye may seek of his Bounty, and that ye may be grateful.

12.author: Transliteration
12.text: Allahu allathee sakhkhara lakumu albahra litajriya alfulku feehi biamrihi walitabtaghoo min fadlihi walaAAallakum tashkuroona

13.author: Ahmed Ali
13.text: He subjugated for you whatsoever is in the heavens and the earth, each and everything. Verily there are signs in this for those who reflect.

13.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
13.text: And has subjected for you all whatever is in the heavens and in the earth, by His command; indeed in this are signs for people who ponder.

13.author: Arberry
13.text: And He has subjected to you what is in the heavens and what is in the earth, all together, from Him. Surely in that are signs for a people who reflect.

13.author: Daryabadi
13.text: And He hath subjected for your sake whatsoever is in the heavens and whatsoever is in the earth, the whole from Himself. Verily herein are signs Unto a people who ponder.

13.author: Hilali & Khan
13.text: And has subjected to you all that is in the heavens and all that is in the earth; it is all as a favour and kindness from Him. Verily, in it are signs for a people who think deeply.

13.author: Itani
13.text: And He placed at your service whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on earth—all is from Him. In that are signs for a people who think.

13.author: Maududi
13.text: He has subjected to you all that is in the heavens and the earth, all being from Him. Verily there are Signs in this for those who reflect.

13.author: Mubarakpuri
13.text: And has subjected to you all that is in the heavens and all that is on the earth; it is all (as a favor and kindness) from Him. Verily, in it are signs for a people who think deeply.

13.author: Pickthall
13.text: And hath made of service unto you whatsoever is in the heavens and whatsoever is in the earth; it is all from Him. Lo! herein verily are portents for a people who reflect.

13.author: Qarai
13.text: He has disposed for you[r benefit] whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth; all is from Him. There are indeed signs in that for a people who reflect.

13.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
13.text: He has subjected to you whatsoever is in the heavens and the earth; all is from Him. Surely, there are signs in this for people who contemplate.

13.author: Saheeh International
13.text: And He has subjected to you whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth - all from Him. Indeed in that are signs for a people who give thought.

13.author: Sarwar
13.text: He has also made subservient to you all that is in the heavens and the earth. In this there is evidence (of the Truth) for those who use their minds.

13.author: Shakir
13.text: And He has made subservient to you whatsoever is in the heavens and whatsoever is in the earth, all, from Himself; most surely there are signs in this for a people who reflect.

13.author: Wahiduddin Khan
13.text: He has subjected whatever is in heaven and on the earth to you; it is all from Him. In that are signs for those who ponder.

13.author: Yusuf Ali
13.text: And He has subjected to you, as from Him, all that is in the heavens and on earth: Behold, in that are Signs indeed for those who reflect.

13.author: Transliteration
13.text: Wasakhkhara lakum ma fee alssamawati wama fee alardi jameeAAan minhu inna fee thalika laayatin liqawmin yatafakkaroona

14.author: Ahmed Ali
14.text: Tell the believers to forgive those who do not fear the visitations of God, so that He may requite the people for their deeds.

14.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
14.text: Tell the believers to ignore those who do not expect the days of Allah, in order that Allah may give a nation the reward of what they used to earn.

14.author: Arberry
14.text: Say unto those who believe, that they forgive those who do not look for the days of God, that He may recompense a people for that they have been earning.

14.author: Daryabadi
14.text: Say thou Unto those who believe, let them forgive those who hope not for the days of Allah, that He may requite a community for that which they have been earning.

14.author: Hilali & Khan
14.text: Say (O Muhammad SAW) to the believers to forgive those who (harm them and) hope not for the Days of Allah (i.e. His Recompense), that He may recompense people according to what they have earned (i.e. to punish these disbelievers, who harm the believers).

14.author: Itani
14.text: Tell those who believe to forgive those who do not hope for the Days of God. He will fully recompense people for whatever they have earned.

14.author: Maududi
14.text: (O Prophet), tell the believers to indulge those who have no fear of any evil days coming upon them from Allah so that Allah may Himself requite them for their deeds.

14.author: Mubarakpuri
14.text: Say to the believers to forgive those who hope not for the Days of Allah, that He may recompense people, according to what they have earned.

14.author: Pickthall
14.text: Tell those who believe to forgive those who hope not for the days of Allah; in order that He may requite folk what they used to earn.

14.author: Qarai
14.text: Say to the faithful to forgive those who do not expect Allah’s days, that He may [Himself] requite every people for what they used to earn.

14.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
14.text: Tell the believers to forgive those who do not look for the Days of Allah, so that He recompenses the people for what they have earned.

14.author: Saheeh International
14.text: Say, [O Muhammad], to those who have believed that they [should] forgive those who expect not the days of Allah so that He may recompense a people for what they used to earn.

14.author: Sarwar
14.text: Tell the believers to forgive those who do not have faith in the days of God (Day of Judgment) and Resurrection. God will give due recompense to all the people for their deeds.

14.author: Shakir
14.text: Say to those who believe (that) they forgive those who do not fear the days of Allah that He may reward a people for what they earn.

14.author: Wahiduddin Khan
14.text: Tell the believers to ignore those who do not believe in the coming of the days of God. He will requite people for what they have done.

14.author: Yusuf Ali
14.text: Tell those who believe, to forgive those who do not look forward to the Days of Allah: It is for Him to recompense (for good or ill) each People according to what they have earned.

14.author: Transliteration
14.text: Qul lillatheena amanoo yaghfiroo lillatheena la yarjoona ayyama Allahi liyajziya qawman bima kanoo yaksiboona

15.author: Ahmed Ali
15.text: He who does good does so for himself; and he who does evil suffers the consequence thereof. You have then to go back to your Lord.

15.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
15.text: Whoever does a good deed, so it is for his own good; and whoever commits evil, does for his own harm; and you will then be returned towards your Lord.

15.author: Arberry
15.text: Whoso does righteousness, it is to his own gain, and whoso does evil, it is to his own loss; then to your Lord you shall be returned.

15.author: Daryabadi
15.text: Whosoever worketh righteously, worketh for himself; and whosoever doth evil, doth against himself; then Unto your Lord ye will be made to return.

15.author: Hilali & Khan
15.text: Whosoever does a good deed, it is for his ownself, and whosoever does evil, it is against (his ownself). Then to your Lord you will be made to return.

15.author: Itani
15.text: Whoever does a good deed, it is for his soul; and whoever commits evil, it is against it; then to your Lord you will be returned.

15.author: Maududi
15.text: Whoever acts righteously, does so to his own good; and whoever commits an evil will suffer its consequence. All of you will then be sent back to your Lord.

15.author: Mubarakpuri
15.text: Whosoever does a good deed, it is for himself, and whosoever does evil, it is against (himself). Then to your Lord you will be made to return.

15.author: Pickthall
15.text: Whoso doeth right, it is for his soul, and whoso doeth wrong, it is against it. And afterward unto your Lord ye will be brought back.

15.author: Qarai
15.text: Whoever acts righteously, it is for his own soul, and whoever does evil, it is to its own detriment, then you will be brought back to your Lord.

15.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
15.text: He who does what is right does it for his own gain, and he who does evil does so at his own loss, then to your Lord you shall all return.

15.author: Saheeh International
15.text: Whoever does a good deed - it is for himself; and whoever does evil - it is against the self. Then to your Lord you will be returned.

15.author: Sarwar
15.text: One who acts righteously does so for his own benefit and one who commits evil does so against his own soul. To your Lord you will all return.

15.author: Shakir
15.text: Whoever does good, it is for his own soul, and whoever does evil, it is against himself; then you shall be brought back to your-- Lord.

15.author: Wahiduddin Khan
15.text: Whoever does what is just and right, does so for his own good; and whoever does evil, does so to his own detriment, and you shall all return to your Lord.

15.author: Yusuf Ali
15.text: If any one does a righteous deed, it ensures to the benefit of his own soul; if he does evil, it works against (his own soul). In the end will ye (all) be brought back to your Lord.

15.author: Transliteration
15.text: Man AAamila salihan falinafsihi waman asaa faAAalayha thumma ila rabbikum turjaAAoona

16.author: Ahmed Ali
16.text: We gave the children of Israel the Book, and the judgement and the prophethood, provided them with good things, favoured them over other people,

16.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
16.text: And indeed We gave the Descendants of Israel the Book, and the rule, and the Prophethood, and gave good things for sustenance and gave them superiority over all others of their time.

16.author: Arberry
16.text: Indeed, We gave the Children of Israel the Book, the Judgment, and the Prophethood, and We provided them with good things, and We preferred them above all beings.

16.author: Daryabadi
16.text: And assuredly. We vouchsafed Unto the Children of Israil the Book and the wisdom and the propherhood, and We provided them with good things and preferred them above the worlds.

16.author: Hilali & Khan
16.text: And indeed We gave the Children of Israel the Scripture, and the understanding of the Scripture and its laws, and the Prophethood; and provided them with good things, and preferred them above the 'Alamin (mankind and jinns) (of their time, during that period),

16.author: Itani
16.text: We gave the Children of Israel the Book, and wisdom, and prophecy; and We provided them with the good things; and We gave them advantage over all other people.

16.author: Maududi
16.text: Indeed We endowed the Children of Israel with the Book and Wisdom and Prophethood, and provided them with good things as sustenance, and exalted them above the peoples of the whole world.

16.author: Mubarakpuri
16.text: And indeed We gave the Children of Israel the Scripture, and the understanding of the Scripture and its laws, and the prophethood; and provided them with good things, and preferred them above the nations.

16.author: Pickthall
16.text: And verily we gave the Children of Israel the Scripture and the Command and the Prophethood, and provided them with good things and favoured them above (all) peoples;

16.author: Qarai
16.text: Certainly We gave the Children of Israel the Book, judgement and prophethood and We provided them with all the good things. We gave them an advantage over all the nations,

16.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
16.text: We gave to the Children of Israel the Book, judgment and prophethood. We provided them with good things and preferred them above the worlds (of theirtime).

16.author: Saheeh International
16.text: And We did certainly give the Children of Israel the Scripture and judgement and prophethood, and We provided them with good things and preferred them over the worlds.

16.author: Sarwar
16.text: We gave the Book to the Israelites, the commandments, and prophethood, granted them pure sustenance, and gave them preference above all people.

16.author: Shakir
16.text: And certainly We gave the Book and the wisdom and the prophecy to the children of Israel, and We gave them of the goodly things, and We made them excel the nations.

16.author: Wahiduddin Khan
16.text: We gave Scriptures, wisdom and prophethood to the Children of Israel, and provided them with good things and favoured them over all other people.

16.author: Yusuf Ali
16.text: We did aforetime grant to the Children of Israel the Book the Power of Command, and Prophethood; We gave them, for Sustenance, things good and pure; and We favoured them above the nations.

16.author: Transliteration
16.text: Walaqad atayna banee israeela alkitaba waalhukma waalnnubuwwata warazaqnahum mina alttayyibati wafaddalnahum AAala alAAalameena

17.author: Ahmed Ali
17.text: And gave them a clear exposition of Our laws. And they did not differ until after knowledge came to them, through mutual jealousies. Verily your Lord will judge between them on the Day of Judgement in what they differed about.

17.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
17.text: And We gave them clear proofs in the command; so they did not differ except after the knowledge had come to them – due to jealousy among themselves; indeed your Lord will judge between them on the Day of Resurrection concerning the matter in which they differ.

17.author: Arberry
17.text: We gave them clear signs of the Command; so they differed not, except after the knowledge had come to them, being insolent one to another. Surely thy Lord will decide between them on the Day of Resurrection touching their differences.

17.author: Daryabadi
17.text: And We vouchsafed Unto them evidences of the affair. And they differed not except after the knowledge had come Unto them, through spite among themselves. Verily thy Lord will decide between them on the DaY of Judgment concerning that wherein they have been differing.

17.author: Hilali & Khan
17.text: And gave them clear proofs in matters [by revealing to them the Taurat (Torah)]. And they differed not until after the knowledge came to them, through envy among themselves. Verily, Your Lord will judge between them on the Day of Resurrection about that wherein they used to differ.

17.author: Itani
17.text: And We gave them precise rulings. They fell into dispute only after knowledge came to them, out of mutual rivalry. Your Lord will judge between them on the Day of Resurrection regarding the things they differed about.

17.author: Maududi
17.text: We gave them clear directions in matters pertaining to religion. Yet they differed among themselves (not out of ignorance but) after knowledge had come to them; and they did so out of the desire to commit excesses against one another. On the Day of Resurrection Allah will judge among them regarding what they had differed.

17.author: Mubarakpuri
17.text: And gave them clear proofs in matters. And they differed not until after the knowledge came to them, through envy among themselves. Verily, your Lord will judge between them on the Day of Resurrection about that wherein they used to differ.

17.author: Pickthall
17.text: And gave them plain commandments. And they differed not until after the knowledge came unto them, through rivalry among themselves. Lo! thy Lord will judge between them on the Day of Resurrection concerning that wherein they used to differ.

17.author: Qarai
17.text: and We gave them manifest precepts. But they did not differ except after knowledge had come to them, out of envy among themselves. Indeed your Lord will judge between them on the Day of Resurrection concerning that about which they used to differ.

17.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
17.text: We gave them clear signs of the Command; yet it was not until after knowledge had come to them that they differed among themselves, and were insolent to each other. On the Day of Resurrection your Lord will indeed decide their differences.

17.author: Saheeh International
17.text: And We gave them clear proofs of the matter [of religion]. And they did not differ except after knowledge had come to them - out of jealous animosity between themselves. Indeed, your Lord will judge between them on the Day of Resurrection concerning that over which they used to differ.

17.author: Sarwar
17.text: We also gave them clear evidence in support of the true religion. Only after having received knowledge did they create differences among themselves because of their rebelliousness. Your Lord will issue His decree concerning their differences on the Day of Judgment.

17.author: Shakir
17.text: And We gave them clear arguments in the affair, but they did not differ until after knowledge had come to them out of envy among themselves; surely your -Lord will judge between them on the day of resurrection concerning that wherein they differed.

17.author: Wahiduddin Khan
17.text: We gave them clear arguments in matters [of religion]. It was only after knowledge came to them that they differed among themselves out of mutual rivalry. On the Day of Resurrection your Lord will judge between them regarding their differences.

17.author: Yusuf Ali
17.text: And We granted them Clear Signs in affairs (of Religion): it was only after knowledge had been granted to them that they fell into schisms, through insolent envy among themselves. Verily thy Lord will judge between them on the Day of Judgment as to those matters in which they set up differences.

17.author: Transliteration
17.text: Waataynahum bayyinatin mina alamri fama ikhtalafoo illa min baAAdi ma jaahumu alAAilmu baghyan baynahum inna rabbaka yaqdee baynahum yawma alqiyamati feema kanoo feehi yakhtalifoona

18.author: Ahmed Ali
18.text: We have put you on the right way in the matter (of divine law). So follow it, and do not follow the wishes of those who are ignorant.

18.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
18.text: We then placed you (O dear prophet Mohammed – peace and blessings be upon him) upon the clear path of the command, therefore continue following it and do not listen to the desires of the ignorant.

18.author: Arberry
18.text: Then We set thee upon an open way of the Command; therefore follow it, and follow not the caprices of those who do not know.

18.author: Daryabadi
18.text: And thereafter We have placed thee upon the law of the religion; so follow it thou, and follow not the vain desires of those who know not.

18.author: Hilali & Khan
18.text: Then We have put you (O Muhammad SAW) on a plain way of (Our) commandment [like the one which We commanded Our Messengers before you (i.e. legal ways and laws of the Islamic Monotheism)]. So follow you that (Islamic Monotheism and its laws), and follow not the desires of those who know not. [Tafsir At-Tabari Vol. 25, Page 146].

18.author: Itani
18.text: Then We set you upon a pathway of faith, so follow it, and do not follow the inclinations of those who do not know.

18.author: Maududi
18.text: And then We set you, (O Prophet), on a clear high road in religious matters. So follow that and do not follow the desires of those who do not know.

18.author: Mubarakpuri
18.text: Then We have put you on a (legal) way of commandment. So follow you that, and follow not the desires of those who know not.

18.author: Pickthall
18.text: And now have We set thee (O Muhammad) on a clear road of (Our) commandment; so follow it, and follow not the whims of those who know not.

18.author: Qarai
18.text: Then We set you upon a clear course of the Law; so follow it, and do not follow the desires of those who do not know.

18.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
18.text: And now, We have set you on the jurisprudence of the Command, therefore follow it and do not follow the desires of those who do not know.

18.author: Saheeh International
18.text: Then We put you, [O Muhammad], on an ordained way concerning the matter [of religion]; so follow it and do not follow the inclinations of those who do not know.

18.author: Sarwar
18.text: We have established for you a code of conduct and a religion. Follow it and do not follow the desires of the ignorant people.

18.author: Shakir
18.text: Then We have made you follow a course in the affair, therefore follow it, and do not follow the low desires of those who do not know.

18.author: Wahiduddin Khan
18.text: Then We set you on a clear path [of religion]: so follow it, and do not yield to the desires of those who have no knowledge.

18.author: Yusuf Ali
18.text: Then We put thee on the (right) Way of Religion: so follow thou that (Way), and follow not the desires of those who know not.

18.author: Transliteration
18.text: Thumma jaAAalnaka AAala shareeAAatin mina alamri faittabiAAha wala tattabiAA ahwaa allatheena la yaAAlamoona

19.author: Ahmed Ali
19.text: They will not avail you in the least against God. Surely the wicked are each other's friends, but God befriends those who fear and follow the right path.

19.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
19.text: Indeed they cannot benefit you at all against Allah; and indeed the unjust are the friends of each other; and Allah is the Friend of the pious.

19.author: Arberry
19.text: Surely they will not avail thee aught against God. Surely the evildoers are friends one of the other; God is the friend of the godfearing.

19.author: Daryabadi
19.text: Verily they cannot avail thee at all against Allah. And verily the wrong doers! friends are they one Unto another. And Allah is the Friend of the God-fearing.

19.author: Hilali & Khan
19.text: Verily, they can avail you nothing against Allah (if He wants to punish you). Verily, the Zalimun (polytheists, wrong-doers, etc.) are Auliya' (protectors, helpers, etc.) to one another, but Allah is the Wali (Helper, Protector, etc.) of the Muttaqun (pious - see V. 2:2).

19.author: Itani
19.text: They will not help you against God in any way. The wrongdoers are allies of one another, while God is the Protector of the righteous.

19.author: Maududi
19.text: Surely they will be of no avail to you against Allah. Indeed the wrong-doers are friends of each other, whereas Allah is the friend of the God-fearing.

19.author: Mubarakpuri
19.text: Verily, they can avail you nothing against Allah. Verily, the wrongdoers are protecting friends of one another, but Allah is the Protector of those who have Taqwa.

19.author: Pickthall
19.text: Lo! they can avail thee naught against Allah. And lo! as for the wrong-doers, some of them are friends of others; and Allah is the Friend of those who ward off (evil).

19.author: Qarai
19.text: Indeed they will not avail you in any way against Allah. Indeed the wrongdoers are allies of one another, but Allah is the ally of the Godwary.

19.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
19.text: Indeed, they will not help you at all against Allah. The harmdoers are guides of each other; but Allah is the Guide of the cautious.

19.author: Saheeh International
19.text: Indeed, they will never avail you against Allah at all. And indeed, the wrongdoers are allies of one another; but Allah is the protector of the righteous.

19.author: Sarwar
19.text: They will never be sufficient (protection) for you in place of God. The unjust are each other's friends, but God is the guardian of the pious ones.

19.author: Shakir
19.text: Surely they shall not avail you in the least against Allah; and surely the unjust are friends of each other, and Allah is the guardian of those who guard (against evil).

19.author: Wahiduddin Khan
19.text: They can be of no avail to you against God. The wrongdoers are friends of one another, while the friend of the righteous is God.

19.author: Yusuf Ali
19.text: They will be of no use to thee in the sight of Allah: it is only Wrong-doers (that stand as) protectors, one to another: but Allah is the Protector of the Righteous.

19.author: Transliteration
19.text: Innahum lan yughnoo AAanka mina Allahi shayan wainna alththalimeena baAAduhum awliyao baAAdin waAllahu waliyyu almuttaqeena

20.author: Ahmed Ali
20.text: These are precepts of wisdom for men, and guidance and grace for people who believe with certainty.

20.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
20.text: This is an enlightenment for mankind, and a guidance and mercy for the people of faith.

20.author: Arberry
20.text: This is clear proofs for men, and a guidance, and a mercy to a people having sure faith.

20.author: Daryabadi
20.text: This Book is an enlightenment Unto mankind and a guidance, and a mercy Unto a People who are convinced.

20.author: Hilali & Khan
20.text: This (Quran) is a clear insight and evidence for mankind, and a guidance and a mercy for people who have Faith with certainty.

20.author: Itani
20.text: This is an illumination for mankind, and guidance, and mercy for people who believe with certainty.

20.author: Maududi
20.text: These are the lights of discernment for people and guidance and mercy for those endowed with sure faith.

20.author: Mubarakpuri
20.text: This is a clear insight and evidence for mankind, and a guidance and a mercy for people who have faith with certainty.

20.author: Pickthall
20.text: This is clear indication for mankind, and a guidance and a mercy for a folk whose faith is sure.

20.author: Qarai
20.text: These are eye-openers for mankind, and guidance and mercy for a people who have certainty.

20.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
20.text: These are clear proofs for people, a guidance, and mercy for those who are certain (of the Resurrection) in belief.

20.author: Saheeh International
20.text: This [Qur'an] is enlightenment for mankind and guidance and mercy for a people who are certain [in faith].

20.author: Sarwar
20.text: This (Quran) is an enlightenment for the people and a guide and mercy for the people who have strong faith.

20.author: Shakir
20.text: These are clear proofs for men, and a guidance and a mercy for a people who are sure.

20.author: Wahiduddin Khan
20.text: This [Book] brings enlightenment and guidance to mankind, and is a blessing for those who have firm faith.

20.author: Yusuf Ali
20.text: These are clear evidences to men and a Guidance and Mercy to those of assured Faith.

20.author: Transliteration
20.text: Hatha basairu lilnnasi wahudan warahmatun liqawmin yooqinoona

21.author: Ahmed Ali
21.text: Do those who seek evil think that We shall make them equal in life and death to those who believe and do good? How bad is the judgement that they make!

21.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
21.text: Do those who commit evil assume that We will make them equal to those who believe and do good deeds, therefore both becoming equal in life and death? What an evil judgement they impose!

21.author: Arberry
21.text: Or do those who commit evil deeds think that We shall make them as those who believe and do righteous deeds, equal their living and their dying? How ill they judge!

21.author: Daryabadi
21.text: Deem those who commit ill deeds that We shall make them as those who believe and work righteous Works? Equal is their life and their death! How ill they judge!

21.author: Hilali & Khan
21.text: Or do those who earn evil deeds think that We shall hold them equal with those who believe (in the Oneness of Allah Islamic Monotheism) and do righteous good deeds, in their present life and after their death? Worst is the judgement that they make.

21.author: Itani
21.text: Do those who perpetrate the evil deeds assume that We will regard them as equal to those who believe and do righteous deeds, whether in their life or their death? Evil is their judgment!

21.author: Maududi
21.text: Do the evil-doers imagine that We shall make them equal to those who believe and do good, making their lives and deaths alike? How vile is their judgement!

21.author: Mubarakpuri
21.text: Or do those who earn evil deeds think that We shall hold them equal with those who believe and do righteous good deeds, in their present life and after their death Worst is the judgement that they make.

21.author: Pickthall
21.text: Or do those who commit ill-deeds suppose that We shall make them as those who believe and do good works, the same in life and death? Bad is their judgment!

21.author: Qarai
21.text: Do those who have perpetrated misdeeds suppose that We shall treat them like those who have faith and do righteous deeds, their life and death being equal? Evil is the judgement that they make!

21.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
21.text: Do those who commit evil deeds think that We will make them (equal) to the believers who do good works, so that in life and death they shall be alike? How evil they judge!

21.author: Saheeh International
21.text: Or do those who commit evils think We will make them like those who have believed and done righteous deeds - [make them] equal in their life and their death? Evil is that which they judge.

21.author: Sarwar
21.text: Do the people who commit evil think that We shall make their life and death like that of the righteously striving believers? How terrible is their Judgment!

21.author: Shakir
21.text: Nay! do those who have wrought evil deeds think that We will make them like those who believe and do good-- that their life and their death shall be equal? Evil it is that they judge.

21.author: Wahiduddin Khan
21.text: Do those who commit evil deeds imagine that We shall deal with them in the same way as We deal with those who have attained to faith and do righteous deeds, that they will be alike in their living and their dying? How badly they judge!

21.author: Yusuf Ali
21.text: What! Do those who seek after evil ways think that We shall hold them equal with those who believe and do righteous deeds,- that equal will be their life and their death? Ill is the judgment that they make.

21.author: Transliteration
21.text: Am hasiba allatheena ijtarahoo alssayyiati an najAAalahum kaallatheena amanoo waAAamiloo alssalihati sawaan mahyahum wamamatuhum saa ma yahkumoona

22.author: Ahmed Ali
22.text: God has created the heavens and the earth with reason, so that He may reward each soul in accordance with what it has done; and no wrong will be done to them.

22.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
22.text: And Allah has created the heavens and the earth with the truth, and so that every soul is repaid for what it has earned, and they will not be wronged.

22.author: Arberry
22.text: God created the heavens and the earth in truth, and that every soul may be recompensed for what it has earned; they shall not be wronged.

22.author: Daryabadi
22.text: And Allah hath created the heavens and the earth with purpose and that every soul may be recompensed for that which it hath earned. And they will not be wronged.

22.author: Hilali & Khan
22.text: And Allah has created the heavens and the earth with truth, in order that each person may be recompensed what he has earned, and they will not be wronged.

22.author: Itani
22.text: God created the heavens and the earth with justice, so that every soul will be repaid for what it has earned. And they will not be wronged.

22.author: Maududi
22.text: Allah created the heavens and the earth in Truth that each person may be requited for his deeds. They shall not be wronged.

22.author: Mubarakpuri
22.text: And Allah has created the heavens and the earth with truth, in order that each person may be recompensed what he has earned, and they will not be wronged.

22.author: Pickthall
22.text: And Allah hath created the heavens and the earth with truth, and that every soul may be repaid what it hath earned. And they will not be wronged.

22.author: Qarai
22.text: Allah created the heavens and the earth with consummate wisdom, so that every soul may be requited for what it has earned, and they will not be wronged.

22.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
22.text: Allah created the heavens and the earth in truth and that each soul shall be recompensed for what it earned, they shall not be wronged.

22.author: Saheeh International
22.text: And Allah created the heavens and earth in truth and so that every soul may be recompensed for what it has earned, and they will not be wronged.

22.author: Sarwar
22.text: God has created the heavens and the earth for a genuine purpose so that every soul will be duly recompensed for its deeds without being wronged.

22.author: Shakir
22.text: And Allah created the heavens and the earth with truth and that every soul may be rewarded for what it has earned and they shall not be wronged.

22.author: Wahiduddin Khan
22.text: God has created the heavens and the earth for a true purpose, so that every soul may be rewarded for whatever it has earned, and no one will be wronged.

22.author: Yusuf Ali
22.text: Allah created the heavens and the earth for just ends, and in order that each soul may find the recompense of what it has earned, and none of them be wronged.

22.author: Transliteration
22.text: Wakhalaqa Allahu alssamawati waalarda bialhaqqi walitujza kullu nafsin bima kasabat wahum la yuthlamoona

23.author: Ahmed Ali
23.text: Just think: Who apart from God can show the way to him who deifies his ego into his god, whom God allows to go astray knowingly, and seals his ears and heart, and covers over his eyes with a veil? Why then do you not contemplate?

23.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
23.text: Just look at him who makes his desire as his God, and Allah has sent him astray despite his having knowledge, and set a seal upon his ears and his heart, and a covering upon his eyes; so who will guide him after Allah? So do you not ponder?

23.author: Arberry
23.text: Hast thou seen him who has taken his caprice to be his god, and God has led him astray out of a knowledge, and set a seal upon his hearing and his heart, and laid a covering on his eyes? Who shall guide him after God? What, will you not remember?

23.author: Daryabadi
23.text: Beholdest thou him who taketh for his god his own vain desire, and Allah hath sent him astray despite his knowledge, and hath sealed up his hearing and his heart, and hath set on his sight a covering? Who will guide him after Allah? Will ye not then be admonished?

23.author: Hilali & Khan
23.text: Have you seen him who takes his own lust (vain desires) as his ilah (god), and Allah knowing (him as such), left him astray, and sealed his hearing and his heart, and put a cover on his sight. Who then will guide him after Allah? Will you not then remember?

23.author: Itani
23.text: Have you considered him who has taken his desire for his god? God has knowingly led him astray, and has sealed his hearing and his heart, and has placed a veil over his vision. Who will guide him after God? Will you not reflect?

23.author: Maududi
23.text: Did you ever consider the case of him who took his desire as his god, and then Allah caused him to go astray despite knowledge, and sealed his hearing and his heart, and cast a veil over his sight? Who, after Allah, can direct him to the Right Way? Will you not take heed?

23.author: Mubarakpuri
23.text: Have you seen him who takes his own lust as his god And Allah, left him astray with knowledge, and sealed his hearing and his heart, and put a cover on his sight. Who then will guide him after Allah Will you not then remember

23.author: Pickthall
23.text: Hast thou seen him who maketh his desire his god, and Allah sendeth him astray purposely, and sealeth up his hearing and his heart, and setteth on his sight a covering? Then who will lead him after Allah (hath condemned him)? Will ye not then heed?

23.author: Qarai
23.text: Have you seen him who has taken his desire to be his god and whom Allah has led astray knowingly, set a seal upon his hearing and heart, and put a blindfold on his sight? So who will guide him after Allah [has consigned him to error]? Will you not then take admonition?

23.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
23.text: Have you seen he who took his desire for a god? With knowledge, Allah has led him astray, setting a seal upon his hearing and heart, and has made a veil over his eyes, who then shall guide him after Allah? Will you not then remember!

23.author: Saheeh International
23.text: Have you seen he who has taken as his god his [own] desire, and Allah has sent him astray due to knowledge and has set a seal upon his hearing and his heart and put over his vision a veil? So who will guide him after Allah? Then will you not be reminded?

23.author: Sarwar
23.text: Have you seen the one who has chosen his desires as his lord? God has knowingly caused him to go astray, sealed his ears and heart and veiled his vision. Who besides God can guide him? Will they, then, not take heed?

23.author: Shakir
23.text: Have you then considered him who takes his low desire for his god, and Allah has made him err having knowledge and has set a seal upon his ear and his heart and put a covering upon his eye. Who can then guide him after Allah? Will you not then be mindful?

23.author: Wahiduddin Khan
23.text: [Prophet], consider the one who has taken his own desire as a deity, whom God allows to stray in the face of knowledge, sealing his ears and heart and covering his eyes -- who can guide such a person after God [has abandoned him]? Will you not take heed?

23.author: Yusuf Ali
23.text: Then seest thou such a one as takes as his god his own vain desire? Allah has, knowing (him as such), left him astray, and sealed his hearing and his heart (and understanding), and put a cover on his sight. Who, then, will guide him after Allah (has withdrawn Guidance)? Will ye not then receive admonition?

23.author: Transliteration
23.text: Afaraayta mani ittakhatha ilahahu hawahu waadallahu Allahu AAala AAilmin wakhatama AAala samAAihi waqalbihi wajaAAala AAala basarihi ghishawatan faman yahdeehi min baAAdi Allahi afala tathakkaroona

24.author: Ahmed Ali
24.text: Yet they say: "There is nothing but the life of this world. We die and we live, and only time annihilates us." Yet they have no knowledge of this: They only speculate.

24.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
24.text: And they said, “There is nothing except our life of this world – we die and we live – and nothing destroys us except the passage of time”; and they do not have any knowledge of it; they only make guesses.

24.author: Arberry
24.text: They say, 'There is nothing but our present life; we die, and we live, and nothing but Time destroys us.' Of that they have no knowledge; they merely conjecture.

24.author: Daryabadi
24.text: And they say: there is no naught but our life of the world; die and we live; and naught destroyeth us save Time. And they have no knowledge thereof: they do but guess.

24.author: Hilali & Khan
24.text: And they say: "There is nothing but our life of this world, we die and we live and nothing destroys us except Ad-Dahr (the time). And they have no knowledge of it, they only conjecture.

24.author: Itani
24.text: And they say, “There is nothing but this our present life; we die and we live, and nothing destroys us except time.” But they have no knowledge of that; they are only guessing.

24.author: Maududi
24.text: They say: “There is no life other than our present worldly life: herein we live and we die, and it is only (the passage of) time that destroys us. Yet the fact is that they know nothing about this and are only conjecturing.

24.author: Mubarakpuri
24.text: And they say: "There is nothing but our life of this world, we die and we live and nothing destroys us except Ad-Dahr (time). And they have no knowledge of it, they only presume.

24.author: Pickthall
24.text: And they say: There is naught but our life of the world; we die and we live, and naught destroyeth us save time; when they have no knowledge whatsoever of (all) that; they do but guess.

24.author: Qarai
24.text: They say, ‘There is nothing but the life of this world: we live and we die, and nothing but time destroys us.’ But they do not have any knowledge of that, and they only make conjectures.

24.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
24.text: They say: 'There is nothing except this life, we die and we live, it is only time that destroys us' Surely, of this they have no knowledge, they are just guessing.

24.author: Saheeh International
24.text: And they say, "There is not but our worldly life; we die and live, and nothing destroys us except time." And they have of that no knowledge; they are only assuming.

24.author: Sarwar
24.text: They have said, "The only life is this worldly life and here we shall live and die. It is only time which will destroy us" They have no knowledge about this. It is only their guesswork.

24.author: Shakir
24.text: And they say: There is nothing but our life in this world; we live and die and nothing destroys us but time, and they have no knowledge of that; they only conjecture.

24.author: Wahiduddin Khan
24.text: They say, "There is nothing but our life in this world: we die, we live, nothing but time destroys us." They have no knowledge of this; they only follow conjecture.

24.author: Yusuf Ali
24.text: And they say: "What is there but our life in this world? We shall die and we live, and nothing but time can destroy us." But of that they have no knowledge: they merely conjecture:

24.author: Transliteration
24.text: Waqaloo ma hiya illa hayatuna alddunya namootu wanahya wama yuhlikuna illa alddahru wama lahum bithalika min AAilmin in hum illa yathunnoona

25.author: Ahmed Ali
25.text: When Our clear revelations are recited to them, their only argument is to say: "Bring our ancestors back, if what you say is true."

25.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
25.text: And when Our clear verses are recited to them, their only argument is, “Bring back our forefathers, if you are truthful!”

25.author: Arberry
25.text: And when Our signs are recited to them, clear signs, their only argument is that they say, 'Bring us our fathers, if you speak truly.'

25.author: Daryabadi
25.text: And when Our manifest revelations are rehearsed Unto them, their argument is no other than that they say bring our fathers, if ye say sooth.

25.author: Hilali & Khan
25.text: And when Our Clear Verses are recited to them, their argument is no other than that they say: "Bring back our (dead) fathers, if you are truthful!"

25.author: Itani
25.text: When Our clarifying Verses are recited to them, their only argument is to say, “Bring back our ancestors, if you are truthful.”

25.author: Maududi
25.text: And when Our Clear Signs are rehearsed to them, their only contention is: “Bring back to us our fathers if you are truthful.”

25.author: Mubarakpuri
25.text: And when Our Clear Ayat are recited to them, their argument is no other than that they say: "Bring back our (dead) fathers, if you are truthful!"

25.author: Pickthall
25.text: And when Our clear revelations are recited unto them their only argument is that they say: Bring (back) our fathers. then, if ye are truthful.

25.author: Qarai
25.text: When Our manifest signs are recited to them, their only argument is to say, ‘Bring our fathers back [to life], if you are truthful.’

25.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
25.text: And when Our verses are recited to them, their only argument is: 'Bring our fathers back to us, if what you say is true'

25.author: Saheeh International
25.text: And when Our verses are recited to them as clear evidences, their argument is only that they say, "Bring [back] our forefathers, if you should be truthful."

25.author: Sarwar
25.text: When Our enlightening revelations are recited to them, their only argument against it is, "Bring our forefathers back to life if what you say is true".

25.author: Shakir
25.text: And when Our clear communications are recited to them, their argument is no other than that they say: Bring our fathers (back) if you are truthful.

25.author: Wahiduddin Khan
25.text: Whenever Our clear revelations are recited to them, their only argument is to say, "Bring back to us our forefathers, if what you say be true."

25.author: Yusuf Ali
25.text: And when Our Clear Signs are rehearsed to them their argument is nothing but this: They say, "Bring (back) our forefathers, if what ye say is true!"

25.author: Transliteration
25.text: Waitha tutla AAalayhim ayatuna bayyinatin ma kana hujjatahum illa an qaloo itoo biabaina in kuntum sadiqeena

26.author: Ahmed Ali
26.text: Say: "God, who gives you life and makes you die, will (raise the dead) then gather you (and your ancestors) together on the Day of Resurrection of which there is no doubt." And yet most men do not understand.

26.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
26.text: Proclaim (O dear Prophet Mohammed – peace and blessings be upon him), “It is Allah Who gives you life, then will give you death, then will gather you all on the Day of Resurrection in which there is no doubt, but most people do not know.”

26.author: Arberry
26.text: Say: 'God gives you life, then makes you die, then He shall gather you to the Day of Resurrection, wherein is no doubt, but most men do not know.'

26.author: Daryabadi
26.text: Say thou: Allah keepeth you alive, then He shall cause you to die, then He shall assemble you on the Day of Resurrection whereof there is no doubt; but most of mankind know not.

26.author: Hilali & Khan
26.text: Say (to them): "Allah gives you life, then causes you to die, then He will assemble you on the Day of Resurrection about which there is no doubt. But most of mankind know not."

26.author: Itani
26.text: Say, “God gives you life, then He makes you die; then He gathers you for the Day of Resurrection, about which there is no doubt. But most people do not know.”

26.author: Maududi
26.text: Tell them, (O Prophet): “It is Allah Who gives you life and then causes you to die, and He it is Who will then bring all of you together on the Day of Resurrection, a Day regarding which there can be no doubt. Yet most people do not know.

26.author: Mubarakpuri
26.text: Say (to them): "Allah gives you life, then causes you to die, then He will assemble you on the Day of Resurrection about which there is no doubt. But most of mankind know not."

26.author: Pickthall
26.text: Say (unto them, O Muhammad): Allah giveth life to you, then causeth you to die, then gathereth you unto the Day of Resurrection whereof there is no doubt. But most of mankind know not.

26.author: Qarai
26.text: Say, ‘It is Allah who gives you life, then He makes you die. Then He will gather you on the Day of Resurrection, in which there is no doubt. But most people do not know.’

26.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
26.text: Say: 'Allah revives you and causes you to die. Then, He shall gather you on the Day of Resurrection, and of this there is no doubt, yet most people do notknow'

26.author: Saheeh International
26.text: Say, "Allah causes you to live, then causes you to die; then He will assemble you for the Day of Resurrection, about which there is no doubt, but most of the people do not know."

26.author: Sarwar
26.text: (Muhammad), say, "It is God who gives you life and causes you to die. He will bring you together on the inevitable Day of Judgment," but most people do not know.

26.author: Shakir
26.text: Say: Allah gives you life, then He makes you die, then will He gather you to the day of resurrection wherein is no doubt, but most people do not know.

26.author: Wahiduddin Khan
26.text: Say, "God gives you life, then causes you to die, and then will gather you together for the Day of Resurrection, about which there is no doubt. But most people do not know it."

26.author: Yusuf Ali
26.text: Say: "It is Allah Who gives you life, then gives you death; then He will gather you together for the Day of Judgment about which there is no doubt": But most men do not understand.

26.author: Transliteration
26.text: Quli Allahu yuhyeekum thumma yumeetukum thumma yajmaAAukum ila yawmi alqiyamati la rayba feehi walakinna akthara alnnasi la yaAAlamoona

27.author: Ahmed Ali
27.text: God's is the kingdom of the heavens and the earth. The day the Hour is proclaimed the liars will be losers.

27.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
27.text: And to Allah only belongs the kingship of the heavens and the earth; and when the Last Day is established – on that day the followers of falsehood will suffer loss.

27.author: Arberry
27.text: To God belongs the Kingdom of the heavens and the earth. And on the day when the Hour is come, upon that day the vain-doers shall lose.

27.author: Daryabadi
27.text: And Allah's is the dominion of the heavens and the earth; and on the Day whereon the Hour arriveth, then shall lose the followers of falsehood.

27.author: Hilali & Khan
27.text: And to Allah belongs the kingdom of the heavens and the earth. And on the Day that the Hour will be established, on that Day the followers of falsehood (polytheists, disbelievers, worshippers of false deities, etc.) shall lose (everything).

27.author: Itani
27.text: To God belongs the kingship of the heavens and the earth. On the Day when the Hour takes place, on that Day the falsifiers will lose.

27.author: Maududi
27.text: Allah's is the kingdom of the heavens and the earth, and on the Day when the Hour (of Resurrection) shall come to pass, the followers of falsehood shall be in utter loss.

27.author: Mubarakpuri
27.text: And to Allah belongs the kingdom of the heavens and the earth. And on the Day that the Hour will be established -- on that Day the followers of falsehood shall lose.

27.author: Pickthall
27.text: And unto Allah belongeth the Sovereignty of the heavens and the earth; and on the day when the Hour riseth, on that day those who follow falsehood will be lost.

27.author: Qarai
27.text: To Allah belongs the kingdom of the heavens and the earth, and when the Hour sets in, the falsifiers will be losers on that day.

27.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
27.text: To Allah belongs the Kingdom in the heavens and the earth. On the Day when the Hour comes, those who disbelieved shall be the losers.

27.author: Saheeh International
27.text: And to Allah belongs the dominion of the heavens and the earth. And the Day the Hour appears - that Day the falsifiers will lose.

27.author: Sarwar
27.text: To God belongs the kingdom of the heavens and the earth. On the day when the Hour of Doom arrives, the followers of falsehood will be lost.

27.author: Shakir
27.text: And Allah's is the kingdom of the heavens and the earth; and on the day when the hour shall come to pass, on that day shall they perish who say false things.

27.author: Wahiduddin Khan
27.text: To God belongs the kingdom of the heavens and the earth; on the Day when the Hour comes, those who follow falsehood will be the losers.

27.author: Yusuf Ali
27.text: To Allah belongs the dominion of the heavens and the earth, and the Day that the Hour of Judgment is established,- that Day will the dealers in Falsehood perish!

27.author: Transliteration
27.text: Walillahi mulku alssamawati waalardi wayawma taqoomu alssaAAatu yawmaithin yakhsaru almubtiloona

28.author: Ahmed Ali
28.text: You will see each community kneeling down; and each community will be summoned to its ledger (of good and evil deeds). You will receive upon that day your recompense for what you had done.

28.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
28.text: And you will see every group down on its knees; every group will be called towards its book of deeds; “This day you will be repaid for what you did.”

28.author: Arberry
