28.text: And thou shalt see every nation hobbling on their knees, every nation being summoned unto its Book: 'Today you shall be recompensed for that you were doing.

28.author: Daryabadi
28.text: And thou shalt behold each community kneeling; each community shall be summoned to its Book. Today, shall be recompensed for that which ye have been working.

28.author: Hilali & Khan
28.text: And you will see each nation humbled to their knees (kneeling), each nation will be called to its Record (of deeds). This Day you shall be recompensed for what you used to do.

28.author: Itani
28.text: You will see every community on its knees; every community will be called to its Book: “Today you are being repaid for what you used to do.

28.author: Maududi
28.text: On that Day you shall see every people fallen on their knees. Every people will be summoned to come forth and see its Record and will be told: “Today you shall be requited for your deeds.

28.author: Mubarakpuri
28.text: And you will see each nation humbled to their knees (Jathiyah), each nation will be called to its Record (of deeds). This Day you shall be recompensed for what you used to do.

28.author: Pickthall
28.text: And thou wilt see each nation crouching, each nation summoned to its record. (And it will be said unto them): This day ye are requited what ye used to do.

28.author: Qarai
28.text: And you will see every nation fallen on its knees. Every nation will be summoned to its book: ‘Today you will be requited for what you used to do.

28.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
28.text: You shall see every nation hobbling on their knees. Each nation shall be summoned to its Book. (And it will be said:) 'This Day, you shall be recompensedfor what you were doing.

28.author: Saheeh International
28.text: And you will see every nation kneeling [from fear]. Every nation will be called to its record [and told], "Today you will be recompensed for what you used to do.

28.author: Sarwar
28.text: You will see all the people kneeling down. Everyone will be summoned to the Book (containing the record of their deeds). They will be told, "On this day you will be recompensed for what you have done".

28.author: Shakir
28.text: And you shall see every nation kneeling down; every nation shall be called to its book: today you shall be rewarded for what you did.

28.author: Wahiduddin Khan
28.text: You will see every people on their knees, every people shall be summoned to its Record [and a voice will say], "Today you will be requited for your deeds.

28.author: Yusuf Ali
28.text: And thou wilt see every sect bowing the knee: Every sect will be called to its Record: "This Day shall ye be recompensed for all that ye did!

28.author: Transliteration
28.text: Watara kulla ommatin jathiyatan kullu ommatin tudAAa ila kitabiha alyawma tujzawna ma kuntum taAAmaloona

29.author: Ahmed Ali
29.text: This, Our record, will speak about you truthfully. We had everything you did recorded in it.

29.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
29.text: “This Book of Ours speaks against you with the truth; indeed We had been recording all what you did.”

29.author: Arberry
29.text: This is Our Book, that speaks against you the truth; We have been registering all that you were doing.'

29.author: Daryabadi
29.text: This Book of Ours speaketh against you with truth; verily We have been setting down whatsoever ye have been working.

29.author: Hilali & Khan
29.text: This Our Record speaks about you with truth. Verily, We were recording what you used to do (i.e. Our angels used to record your deeds).

29.author: Itani
29.text: This Book of Ours speaks about you in truth. We have been transcribing what you have been doing.”

29.author: Maududi
29.text: This is Our Record which bears witness against you with truth; We used to record all what you did.”

29.author: Mubarakpuri
29.text: This Our Record speaks about you with truth. Verily, We were recording what you used to do.

29.author: Pickthall
29.text: This Our Book pronounceth against you with truth. Lo! We have caused (all) that ye did to be recorded.

29.author: Qarai
29.text: This is Our book, which speaks truly against you. Indeed We used to record what you used to do.’

29.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
29.text: This is Our Book that speaks with the truth over you. We have recorded all that you were doing'

29.author: Saheeh International
29.text: This, Our record, speaks about you in truth. Indeed, We were having transcribed whatever you used to do."

29.author: Sarwar
29.text: This is Our Book. It will tell you the truth. We have made a copy of all that you have done.

29.author: Shakir
29.text: This is Our book that speaks against you with justice; surely We wrote what you did,

29.author: Wahiduddin Khan
29.text: This record of ours will declare the truth about you: We have been recording whatever you have been doing."

29.author: Yusuf Ali
29.text: "This Our Record speaks about you with truth: For We were wont to put on Record all that ye did."

29.author: Transliteration
29.text: Hatha kitabuna yantiqu AAalaykum bialhaqqi inna kunna nastansikhu ma kuntum taAAmaloona

30.author: Ahmed Ali
30.text: So, those who believed and did good things will be admitted to His favour by their Lord. This will be a clear triumph.

30.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
30.text: So those who believed and did good deeds – their Lord will admit them into His mercy; this is the clear triumph.

30.author: Arberry
30.text: And as for those who have believed and done deeds of righteousness, their Lord shall admit them into His mercy; that is the manifest triumph.

30.author: Daryabadi
30.text: Then, as for those who believed and worked righteous works, their Lord will cause them to enter into His mercy: that is a manifest achievement.

30.author: Hilali & Khan
30.text: Then, as for those who believed (in the Oneness of Allah Islamic Monotheism) and did righteous good deeds, their Lord will admit them to His Mercy. That will be the evident success.

30.author: Itani
30.text: As for those who believed and did righteous deeds, their Lord will admit them into His mercy. That is the clear triumph.

30.author: Maududi
30.text: As for those who believe and act righteously, their Lord shall admit them to His Mercy. That indeed is the manifest triumph.

30.author: Mubarakpuri
30.text: Then, as for those who believed and did good deeds, their Lord will admit them to His mercy. That will be the evident success.

30.author: Pickthall
30.text: Then, as for those who believed and did good works, their Lord will bring them in unto His mercy. That is the evident triumph.

30.author: Qarai
30.text: As for those who have faith and do righteous deeds, their Lord will admit them into His mercy. That is the manifest success!

30.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
30.text: As for those who have believed and done good works, their Lord will admit them into His Mercy that is a mighty triumph!

30.author: Saheeh International
30.text: So as for those who believed and did righteous deeds, their Lord will admit them into His mercy. That is what is the clear attainment.

30.author: Sarwar
30.text: The Lord will admit the righteously striving, believing people into His mercy. This is certainly a clear victory.

30.author: Shakir
30.text: Then as to those who believed and did good, their Lord will make them enter into His mercy; that is the manifest achievement.

30.author: Wahiduddin Khan
30.text: Those who believed and did good deeds will be admitted by their Lord into His mercy -- that shall be the manifest triumph.

30.author: Yusuf Ali
30.text: Then, as to those who believed and did righteous deeds, their Lord will admit them to His Mercy that will be the achievement for all to see.

30.author: Transliteration
30.text: Faamma allatheena amanoo waAAamiloo alssalihati fayudkhiluhum rabbuhum fee rahmatihi thalika huwa alfawzu almubeenu

31.author: Ahmed Ali
31.text: As for the infidels, (it will be said): "Were not My messages read out to you? But you behaved with self-conceit, and became a sinful people.

31.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
31.text: And to those who disbelieved, it will be said “Was it not that Our verses were recited to you? In response you were haughty and were a guilty people.”

31.author: Arberry
31.text: But as for those who have disbelieved: 'Were not My signs recited to you, and you waxed proud, and were a sinful people?

31.author: Daryabadi
31.text: And as for those who disbelieved: Were not My revelations rehearsed Unto you? but ye were stiff necked, and ye were a people guilty.

31.author: Hilali & Khan
31.text: But as for those who disbelieved (it will be said to them): "Were not Our Verses recited to you? But you were proud, and you were a people who were Mujrimun (polytheists, disbelievers, sinners, criminals)."

31.author: Itani
31.text: But as for those who disbelieved: “Were My revelations not recited to you? But you turned arrogant, and were guilty people.”

31.author: Maududi
31.text: But those who denied the Truth, they shall be told: “Were My Signs not rehearsed to you? But you waxed proud and became a guilty people.”

31.author: Mubarakpuri
31.text: But as for those who disbelieved (it will be said to them): "Were not Our Ayat recited to you But you were proud, and you were a people who were criminals."

31.author: Pickthall
31.text: And as for those who disbelieved (it will be said unto them): Were not Our revelations recited unto you? But ye were scornful and became a guilty folk.

31.author: Qarai
31.text: But as for the faithless, [they will be asked,] ‘Were not My signs recited to you? But you were disdainful, and you were a guilty lot.

31.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
31.text: But, to the unbelievers (it will be said): 'Were My verses not recited to you, but you were proud and were you not a sinful nation'

31.author: Saheeh International
31.text: But as for those who disbelieved, [it will be said], "Were not Our verses recited to you, but you were arrogant and became a people of criminals?

31.author: Sarwar
31.text: To the unbelievers the Lord will say, "Were not Our revelations recited to you and did not you arrogantly reject them? You were a sinful people".

31.author: Shakir
31.text: As to those who disbelieved: What! were not My communications recited to you? But you were proud and you were a guilty people.

31.author: Wahiduddin Khan
31.text: But those who rejected the truth [will be asked], "When My revelations were recited to you, were you not arrogant and did you not persist in wicked deeds?

31.author: Yusuf Ali
31.text: But as to those who rejected Allah, (to them will be said): "Were not Our Signs rehearsed to you? But ye were arrogant, and were a people given to sin!

31.author: Transliteration
31.text: Waamma allatheena kafaroo afalam takun ayatee tutla AAalaykum faistakbartum wakuntum qawman mujrimeena

32.author: Ahmed Ali
32.text: Whenever it was said: 'God's promise is certainly true, and there is no doubt about the Hour,' you replied: 'We know not what the Hour is. We have only a vague idea, but are not certain.'

32.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
32.text: “And when it was said, ‘Indeed Allah’s promise is true and there is no doubt regarding the Last Day”, you used to say, ‘We do not know what the Last Day is – we think it is nothing except an imagination, and we are not convinced.’”

32.author: Arberry
32.text: And when it was said, "God's promise is true, and the Hour, there is no doubt of it," you said; "We know not what the Hour may be; we have only a surmise, and are by no means certain."

32.author: Daryabadi
32.text: And when it was said; verily Allah's promise is, true, and the Hour! there is no doubt thereof, ye said: we know not what the Hour is, we imagine it naught but an opinion, and we are not convinced.

32.author: Hilali & Khan
32.text: And when it was said: "Verily! Allah's Promise is the truth, and there is no doubt about the coming of the Hour," you said; "We know not what is the Hour, we do not think it but as a conjecture, and we have no firm convincing belief (therein)."

32.author: Itani
32.text: And when it was said, “The promise of God is true, and of the Hour there is no doubt,” you said, “We do not know what the Hour is; we think it is only speculation; we are not convinced.”

32.author: Maududi
32.text: And when it was said to them: “Surely Allah's promise is true, and there is no doubt regarding the Hour of Resurrection,” you were wont to say: “We do not know what the Hour (of Resurrection) is. We are simply making conjectures and are not at all certain.”

32.author: Mubarakpuri
32.text: And when it was said: "Verily, Allah's promise is the truth, and there is no doubt about the coming of the Hour," you said: "We know not what is the Hour: we do not think it but conjecture, and we have no firm convincing belief (therein)."

32.author: Pickthall
32.text: And when it was said: Lo! Allah's promise is the truth, and there is no doubt of the Hour's coming, ye said: We know not what the Hour is. We deem it naught but a conjecture, and we are by no means convinced.

32.author: Qarai
32.text: When it was said, ‘‘Allah’s promise is indeed true, and there is no doubt about the Hour,’’ you said, ‘‘We do not know what the Hour is. We know nothing beyond conjectures, and we do not possess any certainty.’’ ’

32.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
32.text: When it was said: 'The promise of Allah is true, and of the Hour there is no doubt' you replied: 'We do not know what the Hour is, we guess, assuming, and we are by no means certain'

32.author: Saheeh International
32.text: And when it was said, 'Indeed, the promise of Allah is truth and the Hour [is coming] - no doubt about it,' you said, 'We know not what is the Hour. We assume only assumption, and we are not convinced.' "

32.author: Sarwar
32.text: When it was said that the promise of God is true and that the Hour would inevitably come, you said, "We do not know what the Hour of Doom is, we are suspicious about it and we are not convinced".

32.author: Shakir
32.text: And when it was said, Surely the promise of Allah is true and as for the hour, there is no doubt about it, you said: We do not know what the hour is; we do not think (that it will come to pass) save a passing thought, and we are not at all sure.

32.author: Wahiduddin Khan
32.text: When it was said to you, "God's promise is true: there is no doubt about the Hour," did you not reply, 'We do not know what the Hour is. We think it to be nothing but a conjecture, and we are not convinced'"

32.author: Yusuf Ali
32.text: "And when it was said that the promise of Allah was true, and that the Hour- there was no doubt about its (coming), ye used to say, 'We know not what is the hour: we only think it is an idea, and we have no firm assurance.'"

32.author: Transliteration
32.text: Waitha qeela inna waAAda Allahi haqqun waalssaAAatu la rayba feeha qultum ma nadree ma alssaAAatu in nathunnu illa thannan wama nahnu bimustayqineena

33.author: Ahmed Ali
33.text: The evil of what they had done will become clear to them, and they will be seized by what they had scorned.

33.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
33.text: And the evils of their deeds appeared to them, and the punishment they used to mock at encompassed them.

33.author: Arberry
33.text: And the evil deeds that they have done shall appear to them, and they shall be encompassed by that they mocked at.

33.author: Daryabadi
33.text: And there will appear Unto them the evils Of that which they had worked, and there will surround them that whereat they had been mocking.

33.author: Hilali & Khan
33.text: And the evil of what they did will appear to them, and they will be completely encircled by that which they used to mock at!

33.author: Itani
33.text: The evils of what they did will become evident to them, and the very thing they ridiculed will haunt them.

33.author: Maududi
33.text: (On that Day) the evil of their deeds will become apparent to them and what they had mocked at will encompass them,

33.author: Mubarakpuri
33.text: And the evil of what they did will appear to them, and that which they used to mock at will completely encircle them.

33.author: Pickthall
33.text: And the evils of what they did will appear unto them, and that which they used to deride will befall them.

33.author: Qarai
33.text: The evils of what they had done will appear to them, and they will be besieged by what they used to deride.

33.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
33.text: The evil of their deeds will appear to them, and they shall be encompassed by that they mocked,

33.author: Saheeh International
33.text: And the evil consequences of what they did will appear to them, and they will be enveloped by what they used to ridicule.

33.author: Sarwar
33.text: Their evil deeds will be revealed to them and (the torment) which they had mocked will surround them.

33.author: Shakir
33.text: And the evil (consequences) of what they did shall become manifest to them and that which they mocked shall encompass them.

33.author: Wahiduddin Khan
33.text: The evil of their actions will then become clear to them. The punishment they mocked will engulf them.

33.author: Yusuf Ali
33.text: Then will appear to them the evil (fruits) of what they did, and they will be completely encircled by that which they used to mock at!

33.author: Transliteration
33.text: Wabada lahum sayyiatu ma AAamiloo wahaqa bihim ma kanoo bihi yastahzioona

34.author: Ahmed Ali
34.text: "We shall ignore you today," they will be told, "as you had forgotten the meeting of this Day. Your dwelling place is Hell, and there is none to save you:

34.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
34.text: And it will be said, “This day We shall forsake you, the way you had forgotten the confronting of this day of yours – and your destination is the fire, and you do not have any supporters.”

34.author: Arberry
34.text: And it shall be said, 'Today We do forget you, even as you forgot the encounter of this your day; and your refuge is the Fire, and you shall have no helpers.

34.author: Daryabadi
34.text: And it will be said: Today shall forget you, even as ye forgat the meeting of this your Day, and your abode will be the Fire, and none ye will have as helpers.

34.author: Hilali & Khan
34.text: And it will be said: "This Day We will forget you as you forgot the Meeting of this Day of yours. And your abode is the Fire, and there is none to help you."

34.author: Itani
34.text: And it will be said, “Today We forget you, as you forgot the encounter of this Day of yours. Your abode is the Fire, and there are no saviors for you.

34.author: Maududi
34.text: and it will be said: “We will forget you today as you forgot the meeting of this Day of yours. The Fire shall now be your abode, and you shall have none to come to your aid.

34.author: Mubarakpuri
34.text: And it will be said: "This Day We will forget you as you forgot the meeting of this Day of yours. And your abode is the Fire, and there is none to help you."

34.author: Pickthall
34.text: And it will be said: This day We forget you, even as ye forgot the meeting of this your day; and your habitation is the Fire, and there is none to help you.

34.author: Qarai
34.text: And it will be said, ‘Today We will forget you, just as you forgot the encounter of this day of yours. The Fire will be your abode, and you will not have any helpers.

34.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
34.text: and it shall be said: 'Today, We will forget you as you yourselves forgot that you would encounter this Day. The Fire shall be your refuge, and none will help you.

34.author: Saheeh International
34.text: And it will be said, "Today We will forget you as you forgot the meeting of this Day of yours, and your refuge is the Fire, and for you there are no helpers.

34.author: Sarwar
34.text: They will be told, "On this day We shall forget you in the same way that you had forgotten your coming into Our presence. Your dwelling will be hell fire and no one will help you.

34.author: Shakir
34.text: And it shall be said: Today We forsake you as you neglected the meeting of this day of yours and your abode is the fire, and there are not for you any helpers:

34.author: Wahiduddin Khan
34.text: It will be said to them, "This Day We shall forget you, as you yourselves forgot that you would meet this Day. Your abode shall be the Fire, and you shall have no helpers.

34.author: Yusuf Ali
34.text: It will also be said: "This Day We will forget you as ye forgot the meeting of this Day of yours! and your abode is the Fire, and no helpers have ye!

34.author: Transliteration
34.text: Waqeela alyawma nansakum kama naseetum liqaa yawmikum hatha wamawakumu alnnaru wama lakum min nasireena

35.author: Ahmed Ali
35.text: This because you laughed at God's revelations and were taken in by the life of the world." So they will neither be taken out of it, nor will be asked to seek God's favour on that day.

35.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
35.text: “This is because you mocked at the signs of Allah, and the worldly life deceived you”; so this day they are not to be removed from the fire, nor will anyone seek amends from them.

35.author: Arberry
35.text: That is for that you took God's signs in mockery, and the present life deluded you.' So today they shall not be brought forth from it, nor will they be suffered to make amends.

35.author: Daryabadi
35.text: That is because ye took the revelations of Allah scoffingly, and there beguiled you the life of the world. Today, therefore, they will not be taken forth therefrom, nor will they be suffered to please Allah.

35.author: Hilali & Khan
35.text: This, because you took the revelations of Allah (this Quran) in mockery, and the life of the world deceived you. So this Day, they shall not be taken out from there (Hell), nor shall they be Yusta'tabun (i.e. they shall not return to the worldly life, so that they repent to Allah, and beg His Pardon for their sins).

35.author: Itani
35.text: That is because you took God’s revelations for a joke, and the worldly life lured you.” So today they will not be brought out of it, and they will not be allowed to repent.

35.author: Maududi
35.text: You reached this end because you made Allah's Signs an object of jest and the life of the world deluded you.” So they shall not be taken out of the Fire nor shall they be asked to make amends (and thus please their Lord).

35.author: Mubarakpuri
35.text: This, because you took the revelations of Allah in mockery, and the life of the world deceived you. So this Day, they shall not be taken out from there (Hell), nor shall they be returned to the worldly life.

35.author: Pickthall
35.text: This, forasmuch as ye made the revelations of Allah a jest, and the life of the world beguiled you. Therefor this day they come not forth from thence, nor can they make amends.

35.author: Qarai
35.text: That is because you took the signs of Allah in derision, and the life of the world had deceived you.’ So today, they will not be brought out of it, nor will they be asked to propitiate [Allah].

35.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
35.text: That is because you took the verses of Allah (the Koran) in mockery, and you were deluded by your worldly life' So that Day they will not be brought outof it; nor shall they be repentant.

35.author: Saheeh International
35.text: That is because you took the verses of Allah in ridicule, and worldly life deluded you." So that Day they will not be removed from it, nor will they be asked to appease [Allah].

35.author: Sarwar
35.text: This is only because you had mocked the revelations of God and the worldly life had deceived you. On this day they will not be taken out of hell, nor will they be granted any favors.

35.author: Shakir
35.text: That is because you took the communications of Allah for a jest and the life of this world deceived you. So on that day they shall not be brought forth from it, nor shall they be granted goodwill.

35.author: Wahiduddin Khan
35.text: That is because you made a mockery of God's revelations and were deluded by the life of this world." Therefore, today they will not be brought out of the Fire, nor will they be allowed to make amends.

35.author: Yusuf Ali
35.text: "This, because ye used to take the Signs of Allah in jest, and the life of the world deceived you:" (From) that Day, therefore, they shall not be taken out thence, nor shall they be received into Grace.

35.author: Transliteration
35.text: Thalikum biannakumu ittakhathtum ayati Allahi huzuwan wagharratkumu alhayatu alddunya faalyawma la yukhrajoona minha wala hum yustaAAtaboona

36.author: Ahmed Ali
36.text: All praise be to God, Lord of the heavens, Lord of the earth, Lord of all the worlds.

36.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
36.text: So all praise is for Allah only, the Lord of the heavens and the Lord of the earth, the Lord of the Creation!

36.author: Arberry
36.text: So to God belongs praise, the Lord of the heavens and the Lord of the earth, Lord of all Being.

36.author: Daryabadi
36.text: All praise, then, Unto Allah, Lord of the heavens and Lord of the earth, Lord of the worlds.

36.author: Hilali & Khan
36.text: So all the praises and thanks are to Allah, the Lord of the heavens and the Lord of the earth, and the Lord of the 'Alamin (mankind, jinns and all that exists).

36.author: Itani
36.text: Praise belongs to God; Lord of the heavens, Lord of the earth, Lord of humanity.

36.author: Maududi
36.text: So all praise be to Allah, the Lord of the heavens, the Lord of the earth, the Lord of the whole Universe.

36.author: Mubarakpuri
36.text: So all praise is due to Allah, the Lord of the heavens and the Lord of the earth, and the Lord of all that exists.

36.author: Pickthall
36.text: Then praise be to Allah, Lord of the heavens and Lord of the earth, the Lord of the Worlds.

36.author: Qarai
36.text: So all praise belongs to Allah, the Lord of the heavens and the Lord of the earth, the Lord of all the worlds.

36.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
36.text: Praise, belongs to Allah, the Lord of the heavens and the earth, the Lord of the Worlds.

36.author: Saheeh International
36.text: Then, to Allah belongs [all] praise - Lord of the heavens and Lord of the earth, Lord of the worlds.

36.author: Sarwar
36.text: It is only God, Lord of the heavens and the earth and Lord of the Universe who deserves all praise.

36.author: Shakir
36.text: Therefore to Allah is due (all) praise, the Lord of the heavens and the Lord of the earth, the Lord of the worlds.

36.author: Wahiduddin Khan
36.text: Praise, then, be to God, Lord of the heavens, and Lord of the earth, the Lord of all the worlds.

36.author: Yusuf Ali
36.text: Then Praise be to Allah, Lord of the heavens and Lord of the earth,- Lord and Cherisher of all the Worlds!

36.author: Transliteration
36.text: Falillahi alhamdu rabbi alssamawati warabbi alardi rabbi alAAalameena

37.author: Ahmed Ali
37.text: His is the supremacy in the heavens and the earth; and He is the all-mighty and all-wise.

37.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
37.text: And for Him only is the Greatness, in the heavens and in the earth; and He only is the Most Honourable, the Wise.

37.author: Arberry
37.text: His is the Domination an the heavens and the earth; He is the All-mighty, the All-wise.

37.author: Daryabadi
37.text: And His alone is the Majesty in the heavens and the earth, and He is the Mighty, the Wise.

37.author: Hilali & Khan
37.text: And His (Alone) is the Majesty in the heavens and the earth, and He is the All-Mighty, the All-Wise.

37.author: Itani
37.text: To Him belongs all supremacy in the heavens and the earth. He is the Majestic, the Wise.

37.author: Maududi
37.text: His is the glory in the heavens and the earth. He is the Most Mighty, the Most Wise.

37.author: Mubarakpuri
37.text: And His is the majesty in the heavens and the earth, and He is Al-`Aziz, Al-Hakim.

37.author: Pickthall
37.text: And unto Him (alone) belongeth Majesty in the heavens and the earth, and He is the Mighty, the Wise.

37.author: Qarai
37.text: To Him belongs all supremacy in the heavens and the earth, and He is the All-mighty, the All-wise.

37.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
37.text: His is the Pride in the heavens and earth. He is the Almighty, the Wise.

37.author: Saheeh International
37.text: And to Him belongs [all] grandeur within the heavens and the earth, and He is the Exalted in Might, the Wise.

37.author: Sarwar
37.text: It is His greatness that dominates the heavens and the earth. He is the Majestic and All-wise.

37.author: Shakir
37.text: And to Him belongs greatness in the heavens and the earth, and He is the Mighty, the Wise.

37.author: Wahiduddin Khan
37.text: All greatness belongs to Him in the heavens and earth. He is the Almighty, the All Wise.

37.author: Yusuf Ali
37.text: To Him be glory throughout the heavens and the earth: and He is Exalted in Power, Full of Wisdom!

37.author: Transliteration
37.text: Walahu alkibriyao fee alssamawati waalardi wahuwa alAAazeezu alhakeemu
